concerns.text: FR-001 requires datasets covering sample sizes '100 to [deferred]'. The upper bound is missing a concrete value or a verifiable reference (e.g., 'up to 100,000 as per Constitution Principle VII'). Without a specific upper limit, the requirement is untestable: an implementer cannot verify if a dataset selection satisfies the 'range' if the range is undefined.

The Edge Case 'Dataset Size Limit' states the system must 'skip the dataset or reduce the fold count' for <100 samples. This is not verifiable as written because it lacks a deterministic rule for *which* action to take. A test cannot pass/fail if the system arbitrarily chooses between two valid paths. The spec must define the decision logic (e.g., 'If n_samples < 100, skip and log; else reduce folds to n_samples/10').

FR-006 mandates execution on 'CPU-only hardware' without defining the hardware constraints (e.g., vCPU count, clock speed). While 'CPU-only' is a constraint, the performance verification (SC-004) relies on a 6-hour limit. Without a defined hardware profile (e.g., 'GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest runner'), the '6-hour' success criterion is not reproducible or measurable across different environments.
